other_observers: Maggie Boswell, Brenda Wright, Coen Dexter, Mark Peterson
description: The bird was the same shape as the American Robins it was associating with, but a little smaller in overall size and bulk. The head and upper back were gray. Most of the back, wings and belly were rufous.  The primaries were gray and black, as was the tail. The bill and legs were a quite bright yellow. The throat was a white background, but with several dark streaks going to the upper breast. The eye was dark, but there was a reasonable sized red orbital ring that was easily observed. I was not sure whether it was a full adult, or a bird born in 2021, as I'm not sure whether the adult plumage fades a little in winter. I did not hear any distinctive calls.  Maggie and I were birding with the finders of the bird: Brenda Wright and Coen Dexter.
